perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Inform ation to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of R ecipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement, unless explicitly permitted by the Discloser in writing. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Inform ation to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of R ecipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient is permitted to disclose Confidential Information to its parent company.
perturbation.explanation: This adds a clause permitting disclosure to the Recipient's parent company. This directly contradicts the restriction on disclosure to third parties specified earlier in the same section, creating uncertainty about which rule applies. Specifically the term 'third parties', is the parent company concidered a third party, or are they not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement, unless explicitly permitted by the Discloser in writing. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Inform ation to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of R ecipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient is permitted to disclose Confidential Information to its parent company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause permitting disclosure to the Recipient's parent company, while the original text and contradicted text restrict disclosure to third parties, creating a contradiction. The change introduces an exception (disclosure to parent company) which conflicts with the general restriction on disclosure to third parties, causing uncertainty about whether the parent company is considered a third party and thus covered by the restriction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause is in a conflicting implication, creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or doc uments of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly re turned within five (5) business days, accompanied by copies of such documentation, after the evaluatio n set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or doc uments of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly re turned within five (5) business days, accompanied by copies of such documentation, after the evaluatio n set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded. However, the Recipient may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, provided it is securely stored and access is strictly controlled.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of allowing the Recipient to keep a copy for archival purposes directly contradicts the initial requirement to return *any* materials. This creates an ambiguity as to whether all materials *must* be returned, or if a copy can be retained, introducing in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or doc uments of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly re turned within five (5) business days, accompanied by copies of such documentation, after the evaluatio n set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded. However, the Recipient may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, provided it is securely stored and access is strictly controlled.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of *any* materials, while the changed text allows the Recipient to retain one copy for archival purposes. This direct contradiction creates an inconsistency regarding whether all materials must be returned, or if a copy can be retained, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Explanation and aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term. The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a peri od of five (5) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term. The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a peri od of five (5) years following the date of this Agreement. However, the obligations related to Export Control as defined in Section 7 will continue indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a clause that extends the Export Control obligations indefinitely, directly contradicting the five-year term mentioned previously in the same section. This in-text contradiction creates an uncertainty of what the actual agreement term is, and when the agreement obligations are done.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term. The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a peri od of five (5) years following the date of this Agreement. However, the obligations related to Export Control as defined in Section 7 will continue indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 8 to include a clause that extends export control obligations indefinitely, which directly contradicts the original five-year term specified earlier in the same section (Section 8). This in-text contradiction creates an uncertainty of what the actual agreement term is, and when the agreement obligations are done. As the Changed text's Clause conflicts with the other Clause in Section 8, this is a clear contradiction based on the Perturbation Explanation and the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the inte rnal laws of the State of New York (without regard to its conflict of law rules), and shall be binding upon the parties hereto in The United States of America and worldwide. The state cour ts within Monroe County New York and/or the federal courts located in Western New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the inte rnal laws of the State of New York (without regard to its conflict of law rules), and shall be binding upon the parties hereto in The United States of America and worldwide. The state cour ts within Monroe County New York and/or the federal courts located in Western New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. However, disputes concerning intellectual property rights will be resolved through arbitration in Geneva, Switzerland.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradictory clause specifying that intellectual property disputes will be resolved through arbitration in Geneva, Switzerland, while the original clause states that the courts in Monroe County New York and/or the federal courts located in Western New York have exclusive jurisdiction. It is now unclear if all disputes go to New York court, or are there exceptions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the inte rnal laws of the State of New York (without regard to its conflict of law rules), and shall be binding upon the parties hereto in The United States of America and worldwide. The state cour ts within Monroe County New York and/or the federal courts located in Western New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. However, disputes concerning intellectual property rights will be resolved through arbitration in Geneva, Switzerland.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the exclusive jurisdiction clause. Initially, the original text stated that state courts within Monroe County New York and/or the federal courts located in Western New York have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate 'any dispute arising out of this Agreement.' The changed text introduces an exception for intellectual property disputes, which will be resolved through arbitration in Geneva, Switzerland. This directly contradicts the original claim of exclusive jurisdiction for all disputes, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same text that was modified.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, R ecipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in la w, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, R ecipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in la w, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages. However, the Discloser's right to injunctive relief is limited to breaches of Section 3 only. For any other section's breach, only monetary damages will be pursued.
perturbation.explanation: This modification places a direct limitation on the Discloser's remedies. While the original text allows for injunctive relief for any breach, the addition restricts injunctive relief to Section 3 breaches only, specifying that only monetary damages are available for breaches of any other sections. This contradicts the earlier statement about injunctive relief being available for *any* breach, causing confusion about what remedies are available for specific breaches. It is creating a contradiction because it now states the injunctive relief, which was said to be an 'other remedy' to be available, will not be avaiable, creating a direct contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, R ecipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in la w, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages. However, the Discloser's right to injunctive relief is limited to breaches of Section 3 only. For any other section's breach, only monetary damages will be pursued.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a limitation to the Discloser's remedies, stating that injunctive relief is limited to breaches of Section 3 only, and for breaches of other sections, only monetary damages will be pursued. This directly contradicts the original text, which states that injunctive relief is available for *any* breach. The contradictory text and the changed text are located in the same section, and the change introduces a direct contradiction to the original statement regarding the availability of injunctive relief for any breach.
